steps.title: Identify the list with internal commas
steps.content: Each of the three languages (COBOL, Java, Clojure) is described by an appositive phrase with internal commas—like “developed by Grace Hopper in 1959”. When list items contain commas, a special punctuation pattern is required.

steps.title: Apply the semicolon rule for lists with internal punctuation
steps.content: In a series where items contain internal commas, use semicolons (not commas) to separate the main items. The pattern is [Item; Item; and Item]. Choice D correctly shows this: “1959; Java, developed by James Gosling in 1995; and Clojure,” uses semicolons after 1959 and 1995 to mark the boundaries between the three languages. Choices A, B, and C fail to use semicolons at these boundaries, instead using commas. That matches choice D — D is correct.
